meta.title: Artifact Hub MCP — Test Dataset
meta.description: Covers single-tool, 2-step chains, multi-step flows, and human-in-the-loop (ambiguous input) scenarios for all 7 MCP tools.

single_tool.label: Happy path — image with all fields

input.title: Pikachu Full Art
input.description: Official Pikachu artwork from the Pokemon franchise

single_tool.notes: Baseline happy-path. Confirms image MIME detection and Vercel Blob upload.

single_tool.label: Missing description — NOT NULL violation
single_tool.notes: description defaults to empty string in execute() — DB constraint never hit.

single_tool.label: Bot-blocked URL — HTTP 403
single_tool.notes: Fandom blocks server-side fetches. Exposes lack of User-Agent header in fetch().

single_tool.label: Unsupported MIME type
single_tool.notes: Validates MIME allowlist (html, image/*, application/pdf only).
single_tool.fixture_note: unsupported_type_url returns 404 before MIME check; use a URL that actually serves a non-allowed MIME type to exercise that branch

single_tool.label: Private visibility

input.title: Private Pikachu
input.description: Should not appear in public list_artifacts

single_tool.notes: Verify private artifacts are excluded from list_artifacts (which filters visibility=public).

single_tool.label: No filters — all public artifacts
single_tool.notes: Baseline. All returned records must have visibility=public.

single_tool.label: Filter by type=image
single_tool.notes: Every returned artifact must be type=image.

single_tool.label: Filter by tag=pokemon
single_tool.notes: Uses Postgres array contains. Verify tags[] column includes 'pokemon'.

single_tool.label: Tag with no matches
single_tool.notes: Tool returns the string 'No artifacts found.' on empty result.

single_tool.label: Limit=1 returns single artifact
single_tool.notes: Most recent artifact only. Confirms ORDER BY created_at DESC + LIMIT.

single_tool.label: Valid UUID — Pikachu
single_tool.notes: Also exposes inputSchema serialization bug: id param may not reach execute() due to empty properties schema.

single_tool.label: Non-existent UUID
single_tool.notes: Should return helpful 'Call list_artifacts()' hint, not a raw DB error.

single_tool.label: Invalid UUID format
single_tool.notes: Zod z.string().uuid() should reject this before DB call.

single_tool.label: Keyword matching title
single_tool.notes: ILIKE match on title column.

single_tool.label: Keyword matching description
single_tool.notes: ILIKE match on description column.

single_tool.label: ILIKE special chars — SQL safety
single_tool.notes: Query is escaped with replace(/[%_]/g, '\\$&'). Verifies % and _ are neutralised.

single_tool.label: Keyword + type filter
single_tool.notes: Combination filter: keyword AND type. All results must be type=image.

single_tool.label: Non-existent artifact_id
single_tool.notes: DB FK violation or empty insert. Should return helpful error, not raw Supabase stack.

input.content: Amazing Pikachu artwork, very clean lines!

single_tool.label: Comment without rating

input.content: Nice image overall, would love a higher resolution version.

single_tool.notes: rating is optional. DB column should allow NULL.

single_tool.label: Content too short — below 10 char minimum
single_tool.notes: z.string().min(10) should reject 'Good' (4 chars).

single_tool.label: Content at exact minimum (10 chars)
single_tool.notes: 'Looks good!' is 11 chars — should pass. Test boundary at exactly 10 chars too.

single_tool.label: Invalid artifact_id

input.content: This artifact does not exist, feedback should fail.

single_tool.notes: FK constraint or empty result. Should give helpful hint.

expected.notes: Returns feedback_summary if set, or 'Add 3 or more comments' prompt if null.

two_step_chains.label: Publish then immediately share

input.title: Chain Test Image
input.description: Published as part of C1 chain test

two_step_chains.validates: Full publish-to-share lifecycle with dynamic ID threading.

two_step_chains.label: Publish then add feedback

input.content: Freshly published and already leaving feedback!

two_step_chains.validates: Feedback can be added immediately after publish (no async delay needed).

two_step_chains.label: List then drill into artifact
two_step_chains.validates: list → get flow. Also surfaces inputSchema serialization bug if id is dropped.

two_step_chains.label: Add feedback then check summary

input.content: This is excellent artwork, very vibrant colors.

expected.notes: Summary only auto-generates in Phase 5. Until then, expect the 'Add 3 or more comments' prompt.

two_step_chains.validates: Feedback → summary trigger. Tests the Phase 5 summarization gate.

two_step_chains.label: List filtered by tag then share
two_step_chains.validates: Tag-filtered browse → share. Confirms tag filter returns correct artifacts.

multi_step_flows.label: Full content lifecycle: publish → 3× feedback → summarize

input.title: Lifecycle Test Artifact
input.description: Used to test the full feedback-to-summary pipeline

input.content: First comment: the colors are really vibrant and eye-catching.
Second comment: background could use more detail.
Third comment: overall a solid piece, would share with friends.

expected.notes: With 3 comments present, Phase 5 should generate a summary. Pre-Phase-5: still returns prompt.

multi_step_flows.validates: End-to-end publish → feedback accumulation → AI summary trigger. Key lifecycle test.

multi_step_flows.label: Discovery flow: list → search → get → share
multi_step_flows.validates: Full browse-discover-inspect-share flow. Mirrors realistic MCP client usage.

multi_step_flows.label: Publish → share → feedback → summarize

input.content: Shared this with my team and they all loved it.

multi_step_flows.validates: Parallel publish+share branch joined with feedback pipeline.

multi_step_flows.label: Multi-artifact: list PDFs → share each → feedback on one

input.content: Reviewed the PDF — well structured and clear content throughout.

multi_step_flows.validates: Type-filtered list feeds a full share+review sub-flow.

human_in_the_loop.label: Publish — no URL provided
human_in_the_loop.ambiguity: No URL given. publish_artifact requires a URL to fetch from.
human_in_the_loop.expected_agent_behavior: Ask: 'What is the URL of the image you want to publish?'
human_in_the_loop.should_not_do: Call publish_artifact with a null or guessed URL.

human_in_the_loop.label: Share — ambiguous artifact reference
human_in_the_loop.ambiguity: Multiple artifacts could match 'pokemon' (e.g. both Pikachu variants if more are added).
human_in_the_loop.expected_agent_behavior: Call list_artifacts or search_artifacts with query='pokemon', then present candidates and ask: 'Which one did you mean?'
human_in_the_loop.should_not_do: Assume the first result and share without confirming.

human_in_the_loop.label: List — user implies private artifacts
human_in_the_loop.user_message: show me all my uploads including private ones
human_in_the_loop.ambiguity: list_artifacts only returns visibility=public. Private artifacts are not exposed.
human_in_the_loop.expected_agent_behavior: Explain the limitation and show public artifacts only. Suggest checking the Artifact Hub UI for private ones.
human_in_the_loop.should_not_do: Silently return only public results as if they are all results.

human_in_the_loop.label: Feedback — no artifact specified
human_in_the_loop.ambiguity: artifact_id is unknown. content is also missing.
human_in_the_loop.expected_agent_behavior: Ask: 'Which artifact would you like to review? And what would you like to say in your comment?'
human_in_the_loop.should_not_do: Call add_feedback with a guessed artifact_id.

human_in_the_loop.label: Feedback — content too vague
human_in_the_loop.user_message: add feedback to the PDF saying it's good
human_in_the_loop.ambiguity: 'it's good' is 9 chars — fails the 10-char minimum. Also, which PDF if multiple exist?
human_in_the_loop.expected_agent_behavior: Ask user to expand the comment (min 10 chars). If only one PDF exists, confirm it; otherwise ask which one.
human_in_the_loop.should_not_do: Submit 'it's good' and let the server reject it, without warning the user first.

human_in_the_loop.label: Share — no artifact context
human_in_the_loop.ambiguity: No prior artifact referenced. 'This' is undefined.
human_in_the_loop.expected_agent_behavior: Ask: 'Which artifact would you like to share? I can list them if you're not sure of the ID.'
human_in_the_loop.should_not_do: Call list_artifacts and pick the first one without asking.

human_in_the_loop.label: Summarize — no artifact specified
human_in_the_loop.expected_agent_behavior: Ask: 'Which artifact's feedback summary would you like? I can list available artifacts.'
human_in_the_loop.should_not_do: Call summarize_feedback with a guessed or default ID.

human_in_the_loop.label: Search — overly broad query
human_in_the_loop.ambiguity: 'stuff' will likely return no results or irrelevant ones.
human_in_the_loop.expected_agent_behavior: Ask: 'Can you be more specific? E.g. a title keyword, file type, or topic.' If user insists, run search_artifacts with query='stuff' and report results honestly.

human_in_the_loop.label: Publish — URL that redirects to unsupported type
human_in_the_loop.ambiguity: URL appears to be a video (.mp4) — not in the allowed MIME list. User may not know this is unsupported.
human_in_the_loop.expected_agent_behavior: Attempt publish_artifact. On 'Unsupported file type' error, explain allowed types (HTML, JPEG, PNG, GIF, WebP, PDF) and ask for a different URL.
human_in_the_loop.should_not_do: Silently fail without explaining why or what is supported.

human_in_the_loop.label: Share — expiry not specified, user wants 'forever'
human_in_the_loop.user_message: share the Pikachu image, make it never expire
human_in_the_loop.ambiguity: share_artifact has a max of 168h (7 days). 'Never expire' is not supported.
human_in_the_loop.expected_agent_behavior: Explain the 7-day maximum. Ask: 'Would you like me to set it to the maximum of 7 days (168 hours)?'
human_in_the_loop.should_not_do: Pass expires_in_hours > 168 (Zod will reject it) or silently cap at 168 without telling the user.
